response:
To find the probability that a student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A we define the difference variable D as X_B minus X_A. Since both distributions are normal the difference D is also normal with a mean of 75 minus 70 which equals 5. The variance of D is the sum of the individual variances so 10 squared plus 12 squared equals 244 and the standard deviation is the square root of 244 which is approximately 15.62. We seek the probability that D is greater than 0. The z-score for 0 is calculated as 0 minus 5 divided by 15.62 which gives approximately -0.32. Looking up this z-score in a standard normal table shows the area to the left is about 0.3745. Therefore the area to the right or the probability that Group B is higher is 1 minus 0.3745 which equals 0.6255.

For the second part we must find the time t where the exponential growth of Group A equals the logistic growth of Group B. The equation is 50 times e to the power of 0.02t equals 100 divided by 1 plus e to the power of negative 0.15 times t minus 5. This requires a numerical solution because an algebraic isolation of t is not possible with standard methods. By testing values we see that at t equals 10 the left side is roughly 61 and the right side is roughly 73. At t equals 15 the left side is roughly 74 and the right side is roughly 88. At t equals 20 the left side is roughly 89 and the right side is roughly 96. The curves intersect between t equals 15 and t equals 20. A more precise calculation shows they meet at approximately t equals 18.2 weeks. This explanation covers the mathematical process used to solve the problem. The force of the pop culture curriculum drives the faster initial growth in Group B while nothing stops the eventual convergence of the two models. The teacher can use this data to understand how the new curriculum impacts student outcomes over time.
